perturbation.original_text: (b) Confidential Information shall not include any information that: (i) is or subsequently becomes publicly available without Receiving Party's breach of any obligation owed Disclosing Party; (ii) became known to Receiving Party prior to Disclosing Party's disclosure of such information to Receiving Party; (iii) became known to Receiving Party from a source other than Disclosing Party other than by the breach of an obligation of confidentiality owed to Disclosing Party; or (iv) is independently developed by Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Confidential Data shall not include any information that: (i) is or subsequently becomes publicly available without Receiving Party's breach of any obligation owed Disclosing Party; (ii) became known to Receiving Party prior to Disclosing Party's disclosure of such information to Receiving Party; (iii) became known to Receiving Party from a source other than Disclosing Party other than by the breach of an obligation of confidentiality owed to Disclosing Party; or (iv) is independently developed by Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' has been replaced with 'Confidential Data'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contract does not define if 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data' are interchangeable. This lack of clarity generates confusion for any enforcement concerning exclusions, specifically about what constitutes an exclusion from confidential treatment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Confidential Data shall not include any information that: (i) is or subsequently becomes publicly available without Receiving Party's breach of any obligation owed Disclosing Party; (ii) became known to Receiving Party prior to Disclosing Party's disclosure of such information to Receiving Party; (iii) became known to Receiving Party from a source other than Disclosing Party other than by the breach of an obligation of confidentiality owed to Disclosing Party; or (iv) is independently developed by Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data'. The contradicted text also uses the term 'Confidential Data'. The explanation states that the contract doesn't define if 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data' are interchangeable, creating confusion regarding exclusions. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, which states that in-text contradictions occur when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: (c) "Confidential Materials" shall mean all tangible materials containing Confidential Information, including without limitation written or printed documents and computer disks or tapes, whether machine or user readable.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) "Confidential Materials" shall mean all tangible materials containing Protected Information, including without limitation written or printed documents and computer disks or tapes, whether machine or user readable.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' has been replaced with 'Protected Information' in this definition of 'Confidential Materials'. This change creates a contradiction since 'Protected Information' is not defined within the contract, causing ambiguity about what constitutes confidential materials and thus, the scope of protection afforded, therefore any claim on materials can not be enforceable without clarity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) "Confidential Materials" shall mean all tangible materials containing Protected Information, including without limitation written or printed documents and computer disks or tapes, whether machine or user readable.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Materials' in terms of 'Confidential Information'. The changed text redefines 'Confidential Materials' in terms of 'Protected Information', which is undefined in the contract. The contradicted text uses the new definition of 'Confidential Materials', which is in terms of the undefined 'Protected Information'. This change creates a contradiction because it makes it unclear what 'Confidential Materials' encompasses, leading to ambiguity and enforceability issues, as explained in the perturbation explanation. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.
